title: Patterns on the Multiplication Table
essential_question: What patterns can you find on a multiplication table?
strategies: Rows and columns show skip counting

pretest.question: On a multiplication table, what is the product where row 3 and column 4 meet?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.7 'Patterns on the Multiplication Table' pp.163-166

pretest.question: Is the product of 2 × 7 even or odd?
pretest.explanation: 2 × 7 = 14. Any product with an even factor is even.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.7 'Patterns on the Multiplication Table' pp.163-166
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 3 Topic G — Multiplication Patterns and Tables
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: The Multiplication Table
learn.content: A multiplication table shows products of factors. Row 3 shows: 3, 6, 9, 12, 15, 18, 21, 24, 27, 30. Column 3 shows the same numbers — because of the Commutative Property.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.7 'Patterns on the Multiplication Table' pp.163-166
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 3 Topic G — Multiplication Patterns and Tables
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Even and Odd Patterns
learn.content: Even × even = even. Even × odd = even. Odd × odd = odd. Rule of thumb: if at least one factor is even, the product is even.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.7 'Patterns on the Multiplication Table' pp.163-166
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 3 Topic G — Multiplication Patterns and Tables
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Square Numbers on the Diagonal
learn.content: Going diagonally through the table: 1, 4, 9, 16, 25, 36, 49, 64, 81, 100. These are square numbers — a number times itself (n × n).

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.7 'Patterns on the Multiplication Table' pp.163-166
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 3 Topic G — Multiplication Patterns and Tables
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: The 9s Trick
learn.content: Look at the 9s column: 9, 18, 27, 36, 45, 54, 63, 72, 81. The tens digit goes UP by 1; the ones digit goes DOWN by 1. And the two digits always add to 9!

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.7 'Patterns on the Multiplication Table' pp.163-166
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 3 Topic G — Multiplication Patterns and Tables
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Each Row Is Skip Counting
learn.content: Row n is just skip counting by n. Row 4: 4, 8, 12, 16, 20, 24, 28, 32, 36, 40. Every row grows by its row number.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.7 'Patterns on the Multiplication Table' pp.163-166
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 3 Topic G — Multiplication Patterns and Tables
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 짝수·홀수 곱셈 규칙
learn.content: 곱셈표에서 곱의 짝/홀 패턴은 단순합니다: 짝수 × 어떤 수 = 짝수 (예: 2×7=14, 6×3=18). 홀수 × 홀수 = 홀수 (예: 3×5=15, 7×9=63). 이유: 짝수에는 2가 인자에 포함됨 → 짝의 배수. 홀수×홀수만 홀수가 나옴 — 두 인자 모두 2가 없어야 곱도 2가 없음. 흔한 실수: '홀수 + 짝수 = 홀수' 규칙(덧셈)을 곱셈에 잘못 적용.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.7 'Patterns on the Multiplication Table' pp.163-166
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 3 Topic G — Multiplication Patterns and Tables
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 대각선 = 제곱수 (square numbers)
learn.content: 곱셈표의 왼쪽 위에서 오른쪽 아래로 가는 대각선: 1, 4, 9, 16, 25, 36, 49, 64, 81, 100. 각 값은 같은 수를 두 번 곱한 것 — 1×1, 2×2, 3×3,... 이 수들을 '제곱수(square number)'라 부름 — 정사각형 배열을 만드므로. 예: 9 = 3 × 3 → 3행 × 3열의 정사각형 9개. 흔한 실수: 더블(2배)과 제곱(자기 자신 ×) 혼동.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.7 'Patterns on the Multiplication Table' pp.163-166
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 3 Topic G — Multiplication Patterns and Tables
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 곱셈표 패턴 6가지 요약
learn.content: 1) 행/열 교환: 행 a 열 b = 행 b 열 a (교환법칙으로 표는 대각 대칭). 2) ×5 행: 끝자리 0/5 반복 (5,10,15,...). 3) ×10 행: 끝자리 항상 0 (10,20,30,...). 4) ×9 행: 자릿수 합 항상 9 (18→1+8=9, 27→2+7=9). 5) 대각선: 제곱수 (1, 4, 9, 16,...). 6) 짝/홀: 둘 다 홀수일 때만 곱이 홀수. 활용: 모르는 사실을 패턴으로 점검 가능 (예: 7 × 8 = 짝수가 맞는지).

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.7 'Patterns on the Multiplication Table' pp.163-166
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 3 Topic G — Multiplication Patterns and Tables
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

try.question: Which product is odd?
try.explanation: Only 3 × 9 has two odd factors. Odd × odd = odd (27).
hints: Odd × odd = odd. Any even factor makes it even.
Look for both factors being odd.

feedback.incorrect: Only 3 × 9 has two odd factors → odd product.

practice_r2.question: Even × Odd = ?
practice_r2.explanation: Any time a factor is even, the product is even.
hints: Is there an even factor in Even × Odd?
Yes — so the product must be even.

feedback.correct: Right — an even factor forces an even product.

practice_r3.question: How many products on a 1–10 multiplication table are odd?
practice_r3.explanation: Odd × odd = odd. There are 5 odd factors (1, 3, 5, 7, 9). 5 × 5 = 25 odd products.
hints: Odd × odd is the only way to get odd.

feedback.incorrect: 5 odd factors × 5 odd factors = 25 odd products.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.7 'Patterns on the Multiplication Table' pp.163-166
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 3 Topic G — Multiplication Patterns and Tables
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

practice_r3.question: Emma notices: 7 × 6 = 42 and 6 × 7 = 42. On a 10 × 10 table, about what fraction of products are duplicated because of this?
practice_r3.explanation: Because of the Commutative Property, the table is symmetric across the diagonal. Everything off the diagonal has a partner — 45 pairs.
hints: Count pairs above and below the diagonal.

feedback.incorrect: Commutative makes the table symmetric. 45 off-diagonal pairs.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.7 'Patterns on the Multiplication Table' pp.163-166
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 3 Topic G — Multiplication Patterns and Tables
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

practice_r3.question: Type the answer: How many even products are in the 6s row (from 6×1 to 6×10)? ___
practice_r3.explanation: 6 is even, so every product in the 6s row is even. All 10 are even.
hints: Is 6 even? Then every ×6 product is even.

feedback.incorrect: 6 is even, so every ×6 product is even. All 10.
